mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes > 0.4pp, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series are above trend, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 6-month trend signals weakening manufacturing output, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day returns during tightening cycles, or the predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.4% signals accelerating price pressures that force equity investors to price in higher future discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-negative in > 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal shows zero predictive IC across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending capacity and supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no positive correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and declining retail sales indicates a demand shock that compresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth < 2.0% and RSAFS MoM growth < 0.5%
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals strengthening economic momentum, which increases equity risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the predictive accuracy falls below 50% in walk-forward validation.
